summary: Small multi-system decomp matching PR that finished 9 functions and improved 11 more, according to the decomp.dev report: matched code rose to 55.19% (+0.05%, +2064 bytes). The changes are mostly source-shaping edits: stack padding, declaration order, direct field access, prototype width fixes, branch/cast tweaks, and removal of extra stores. The PR body was empty; intent is inferred from the title, diff, and match report.
what_changed: Finished `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238` by reshaping locals and control flow: `Ground* gp` now comes from `gobj->user_data`, `jobj` is declared separately, and redundant-looking null branches were introduced to match codegen.
Finished `ftKb_SpecialN_800F598C` by replacing `mpUpdateFloorSkip(&fp->coll_data)` with `ftKb_SpecialS_800F6BB0(gobj)` before applying Kirby stop momentum.
Finished Kirby `ftKb_SkSpecialNCancel_Coll` and `ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Coll` by adding `PAD_STACK` and changing the `ftCo_80096900` float argument from `0.0F` to `1.0F`.

Improved Kirby special-N functions with stack padding and declaration/register-shaping edits, including `ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim`, `ftKb_MsSpecialNEnd_Anim`, `ftKb_MsSpecialAirNEnd_Anim`, and `ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_Anim`.
Adjusted `ftColl_8007BBCC` to switch on `DmgLogEntry.x0` instead of `DmgLogEntry.kind`, a small struct-field/codegen improvement.
Changed `gm_80164330` and its header prototype from `u8` to `s32`, then added explicit `(u8)` and `(u16)` casts around table indexing and stage-mask shifts.
In `grPura_80211D00`, added static sbss global `grPu_804D6AA0` and stored the return from `Ground_801C49F8()` there, improving the function and `.sbss` match.
Finished `un_80301634` in `soundtest.c` by including `placeholder.h`, adding `PAD_STACK(8)`, and removing an explicit `gobj->user_data = NULL` store.
Adjusted Link boomerang angle normalization by using `f64 angle` and flipping the `facing_dir == 1.0f` branch shape.
Changed `lb_8001B760` from `void` to `int` in both `lbcardnew.c` and `lbcardnew.h`, returning the final result after polling.
Collapsed repeated `lbcardnew` polling loops into assignment-in-condition `do { } while ((result = lb_8001B6F8()) == 0xB);` forms and changed one local result type to `s32`.
smart_moves: Used `PAD_STACK` instead of ad-hoc volatile padding, and added `placeholder.h` where needed for the macro.
Synchronized header and implementation when changing `lb_8001B760` return type, avoiding a prototype mismatch.
Matched target integer-promotion behavior in `gm_80164330` with a wider `s32` parameter plus explicit narrower casts at the actual use sites.
Preferred direct field access (`gobj->user_data`) over a helper macro in `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238` when the macro-shaped code produced the wrong register allocation.
Accepted source shapes that look redundant at C level, such as null checks inside an already non-null branch, because they improved assembly matching.
Removed an apparently unnecessary `gobj->user_data = NULL` store in `soundtest.c`, showing that fewer source operations can be required for a match.
Captured `Ground_801C49F8()` into a named static global in `grpura.c`, improving both function code and sbss data matching.
decomp_lessons: Tiny local-order, cast-width, and stack-padding changes can move functions from mid-90% to 100% without broad rewrites.
For matching, prototype parameter width matters independently from logical domain size; a stage index can still need an `s32` parameter with explicit narrow casts later.
Repeated polling loops may need the assignment inside the `while` condition to match the original instruction schedule.
Do not assume helper macros preserve match quality; direct struct field access can produce different register allocation.
When struct understanding is incomplete, offset-style fields such as `x0` may be more match-stable than premature semantic names like `kind`.
A stale-looking local or sbss object can be real evidence of the original binary storing a returned object globally.
Double precision temporaries (`f64`) versus single precision temporaries (`f32`) are important in item math matches.
Removing extra high-level initialization stores can be as important as adding missing operations.
naming_conventions: New static stage global follows project address-prefix style: `/* 4D6AA0 */ static HSD_GObj* grPu_804D6AA0;`.
Headers retain address comments beside unresolved symbol names, e.g. `/* 164330 */ bool gm_80164330(s32);` and `/* 01B760 */ int lb_8001B760(int result);`.
Unresolved functions remain address-based (`gm_80164330`, `lb_8001B760`, `un_80301634`) when no stronger semantic name is established.
Offset-style struct fields such as `DmgLogEntry.x0` are acceptable when semantic field names hurt evidence-grounded matching.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, and `PAD_STACK(24)` were used across Kirby and soundtest functions to shape stack frames.
Variable declaration order was adjusted in `ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim` and `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238` to influence register allocation.
Pointer aliasing was introduced in `ftKb_SkSpecialAirNLoop_Anim` by initializing `Fighter* new_var = fp` and using it for `ft_PlaySFX`.
Explicit casts to `(u8)` for table indexing and `(u16)` for bit shifts were used in `gm_80164330`.
Assignment-in-condition polling loops were reused in multiple `lbcardnew` functions for result code `0xB`.
A redundant-looking null-check pattern around `root_jobj` and `jobj` was kept because it completed `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238`.
`f64` was used for Link boomerang angle normalization where `f32` did not match.
review_feedback: decomp.dev reported 9 new matches and 11 improvements, including 100% for `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238`, `it_802A15EC`, `it_802A2320`, `lb_8001B8C8`, `un_80301634`, `lb_8001B760`, `ftKb_SpecialN_800F598C`, `ftKb_SkSpecialNCancel_Coll`, and `ftKb_SkSpecialNEnd_Coll`.
Author malvarezcastillo noted: "I can revert the smaller gains if they're just noise / fake matches".
There were no inline review comments in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Check whether the `grBigBlueRoute_8020C238` todo comment claiming a 96% match remained after this PR despite the decomp.dev 100% report.
Search for other `lbcardnew` result-code `0xB` polling loops that may need assignment-in-condition reshaping.
Audit callers of `lb_8001B760` after its return type changed from `void` to `int`.
Revisit `DmgLogEntry.x0` versus `kind` once the damage log struct is better understood.
Look for other stage-index APIs where parameter width should be `s32` but individual uses require explicit `u8` or `u16` casts.
Compare other item angle-normalization helpers for `f64` temporaries and `facing_dir` branch direction.
